# Vintage Rides USA

> Premium adventure motorcycle rental in Rapid City, South Dakota. A fleet of 10 brand-new 2025 Royal Enfield Himalayan 450s available May through September for self-guided rides across the Black Hills, Badlands, Needles Highway, Mount Rushmore, Spearfish Canyon, Custer State Park, and Sturgis.

Vintage Rides USA is the US subsidiary of Vintage Rides, a premium guided motorcycle touring company with 20 years of experience operating in the Himalayas, Andes, and Atlas Mountains. The Rapid City shop is the company's first peer-to-peer rental operation in North America and is an official Royal Enfield partner.

## Key facts

- **Business**: Vintage Rides USA (subsidiary of Vintage Rides)
- **Location**: 1715 Samco Rd, Rapid City, SD 57702, United States
- **Pickup hours**: 9:00 AM (return by 9:00 AM on end date)
- **Season**: May 1 – September 30 (closed October–April)
- **Fleet**: 10 × 2025 Royal Enfield Himalayan 450 (brand new)
- **Price**: $130 USD per day + 11.9% South Dakota tax
- **Minimum rental**: 1 day (multi-day strongly recommended for the loop)
- **Booking**: online at https://www.vintageridesusa.com/book
- **Phone**: +1 (605) 646-2343
- **WhatsApp**: +1 (760) 350-9700
- **Email**: wendy@vintagerides.travel
- **Manager**: Wendy (US subsidiary director)

## Best routes from Rapid City

- **Black Hills loop** (1–3 days): Rapid City → Mount Rushmore → Custer State Park → Needles Highway → Spearfish Canyon → return. ~250–350 miles depending on extensions.
- **Badlands National Park** (1 day): Rapid City → Badlands Loop Road (SD-240) → Wall → return. ~120 miles.
- **Needles Highway + Iron Mountain Road** (half-day or full): twisty granite spires + pigtail bridges + tunnels framing Mount Rushmore.
- **Sturgis loop**: Rapid City → Sturgis → Spearfish → Deadwood → return. Especially relevant during Sturgis Motorcycle Rally (early August, 250k+ riders annually).
- **Devils Tower extension** (multi-day): cross into Wyoming for the iconic monolith.

## Why a Royal Enfield Himalayan 450 in the Black Hills

- Light enough for tight switchbacks (Needles Highway, Iron Mountain pigtails)
- Long-travel suspension absorbs Badlands gravel roads and BLM tracks
- Single-cylinder 452cc engine, around 40 hp — relaxed cruising on Black Hills speeds (45–65 mph)
- Comfortable for full-day riding; adventure-tourer geometry
- Very different from the typical Sturgis Harley experience: lighter, more agile, made for exploring rather than parading

## Documents required to rent

- Valid motorcycle license (US M-class endorsement or international equivalent / IDP)
- Credit card for security deposit
- Riders must be 25 years or older
- All riders sign a liability waiver and rental agreement at pickup

## Booking process

1. Choose dates and number of bikes at https://www.vintageridesusa.com/book
2. Pay securely via Stripe (full amount up front)
3. Receive an instant confirmation email with pickup details and the pickup address
4. Show up at 1715 Samco Rd, Rapid City, SD at 9:00 AM on your start date
5. Return the bike by 9:00 AM on your end date

## What's included

- Brand-new 2025 Royal Enfield Himalayan 450
- Helmet (full-face) on request — bring your own if preferred
- Free GPX route files for the Black Hills loop, Badlands, and Needles Highway
- Itinerary advice from a team that has guided Royal Enfield trips for 20 years
- Roadside support contact during business hours

## What's not included

- Fuel (return with the same fuel level)
- Riding gear beyond helmet (bring your own jacket, gloves, boots)
- Personal accident insurance (recommended via your travel insurance)
- Tolls, park entry fees, lodging, and meals

## Cancellation

- Free cancellation more than 14 days before start date (full refund)
- 50% refund 7–14 days before
- No refund within 7 days of start date
- Reschedule once for free if requested 7+ days in advance

## How Vintage Rides USA differs from competitors

- **vs EagleRider**: smaller fleet, single-model focus on adventure bikes, founder-led operation, GPX routes included free, lower starting price ($130 vs $150–250/day)
- **vs Riders Share / Twisted Road**: dedicated shop with fixed pickup location and full-time staff (not peer-to-peer), 10 identical brand-new 2025 bikes (consistent quality), included GPX routes and itinerary advice
- **vs Harley rental shops**: lighter, more nimble adventure bikes designed for the gravel sections and tight curves of the Black Hills and Badlands, not just paved interstates

## Brand and parent company

Vintage Rides was founded in France in 2005 and operates premium guided motorcycle tours across India, Bhutan, Nepal, Mongolia, Argentina, Peru, Bolivia, Madagascar, Morocco, and several other destinations. The company has 20+ years of experience curating motorcycle adventures and runs a fleet of Royal Enfield bikes globally. Vintage Rides USA is the US subsidiary launched to make this expertise available to North American riders on home soil.
